perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information” means any and all information and know-how that the Discloser and/or its Group Parties and/or its agents may from time to time disclose to the Recipient or the Recipient’s MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT ANNEX 2 SAMPLE NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT I ELAN MOBILE MONEY ASSESSMENT AND CONTRACTING GUIDE 3 Group Parties during the course of the Matter or which otherwise becomes known to the Recipient either during the course of the Matter or during the course of any previous discussions, negotiations or relationship with the Discloser in relation to the Matter, whether orally, in writing, digitally, in the form of machine readable code or embodied in hardware or any other physical medium which relates to the business, including but not limited to the business plan of the Discloser or their Group Parties, data, know-how, designs, illustrations, drawings, photographs, illustrations, notes, memoranda, terms of business, financial information, financial projections, financial records, customers and suppliers, sales and marketing information, spreadsheets, specifications, technical information and computer software of the Discloser and/or its Group Parties
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information” means only written information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' at the time of disclosure. It does not include any orally disclosed information, regardless of whether it is later documented, or any information a reasonable person would consider public knowledge. Any and all information and know-how that the Discloser and/or its Group Parties and/or its agents may from time to time disclose to the Recipient or the Recipient’s MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT ANNEX 2 SAMPLE NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT I ELAN MOBILE MONEY ASSESSMENT AND CONTRACTING GUIDE 3 Group Parties during the course of the Matter or which otherwise becomes known to the Recipient either during the course of the Matter or during the course of any previous discussions, negotiations or relationship with the Discloser in relation to the Matter, whether orally, in writing, digitally, in the form of machine readable code or embodied in hardware or any other physical medium which relates to the business, including but not limited to the business plan of the Discloser or their Group Parties, data, know-how, designs, illustrations, drawings, photographs, illustrations, notes, memoranda, terms of business, financial information, financial projections, financial records, customers and suppliers, sales and marketing information, spreadsheets, specifications, technical information and computer software of the Discloser and/or its Group Parties
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by providing a restrictive definition of 'Confidential Information' (only written and marked) immediately followed by the original, broader definition (any information disclosed orally, in writing, etc.). This creates confusion about what information is actually protected by the NDA. Because Section 3 refers back to the definition of 'Confidential Information' to determine the scope of obligations, this in-text contradiction makes it unclear which definition applies. Section 3’s obligations are therefore made ambiguious.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees: a. All Confidential Information acquired by either Party from the other Party shall be and shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party or the member of its group providing it; b. To hold all Confidential Information in confidence and to procure that its representatives, advisers and associates hold all such Confidential Information in confidence. ANNEX 2 SAMPLE NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT I ELAN MOBILE MONEY ASSESSMENT AND CONTRACTING GUIDE 2 c. To limit access to such Confidential Information to authorised individuals (the “Permitted Personnel”) who have a need to know the Confidential Information in order for the Party to participate in the Matter. d. To procure that its Permitted Personnel are aware and adhere to the terms of this Agreement; e. To use such Confidential Information only for purposes of work, services or analysis related to the Matter and for other purposes only upon such terms as may be agreed upon between the Parties in writing; and f. To return promptly to the disclosing party, or to destroy any copies of such Confidential Information in written, graphic or other tangible form at that Party’s request and any such destruction shall be certified in writing to the disclosing Party by an authorised officer of the Recipient; g. The obligations of each of the Parties with respect to Confidential Information shall extend for a period of three (3) years following the date of disclosure of that Confidential Information; provided, however, that confidentiality obligations herein shall survive for a period of three (3) years from the date of termination of this Agreement; and h. Neither disclosure of Confidential Information, nor this Agreement shall be construed as (i) requiring a Party to disclose or accept Confidential Information, (ii) permitting a Party to use or sell the Confidential Information or products derived therefrom, or (iii) granting to either Party any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, under any patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret or other intellectual property right now or hereafter obtained or licensable by the other Party, or (iv) a representation or warranty by either Party as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information (other than as expressly set out in any agreement subsequently entered into by the Parties).
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a restrictive definition of 'Confidential Information' (only written and marked) at the beginning, but then immediately follows with the original, broader definition. This directly contradicts the section that defines 'Confidential Information'. Because the Contradicted Text, Section 3, refers back to the definition of 'Confidential Information' to determine the scope of obligations, this in-text contradiction makes it unclear which definition applies, and therefore makes Section 3’s obligations ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereby consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and consent that all personal service of process be made by certified mail or registered mail directed to the address stated herein and service so made shall be deemed to be completed upon actual receipt thereof. The Parties waive any objection to jurisdiction and venue of any action instituted against it in England, and agree not to assert any defense based on lack of jurisdiction or venue.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties hereby consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and consent that all personal service of process be made by email to legal@example.com and service so made shall be deemed to be completed immediately upon sending thereof. The Parties waive any objection to jurisdiction and venue of any action instituted against it in England, and agree not to assert any defense based on lack of jurisdiction or venue.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction because Section 7 states that all personal service of process be made by email to legal@example.com and service so made shall be deemed to be completed immediately upon sending thereof, but Section 14 states that The Parties intend to sign and deliver this Agreement by facsimile transmission. Each Party agrees that the delivery of the Agreement by facsimile shall have the same force and effect as delivery of original signatures and that each Party may use such facsimile signatures as evidence of the execution and delivery of the Agreement by all Parties to the same extent that an original signature could be used. The modification creates an in-text contradiction regarding the method of service. One section specifies email, while another assumes the use of facsimile transmission. This conflict raises uncertainty over which service method is legally binding, especially given that section 14 is referring to the transmission of the signatures, so it can be argued that the service should be sent via Facsimile transmission. In essence, section 7 and 14 are in direct conflict regarding the delivery method of agreement documents and signatures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Counterparts and Facsimile Delivery. This Agreement may be executed in two or more identical counterparts, each of which shall be deemed to be an original and all of which taken together shall be deemed to constitute the Agreement when a duly authorised representative of each Party has signed a counterpart. The Parties intend to sign and deliver this Agreement by facsimile transmission. Each Party agrees that the delivery of the Agreement by facsimile shall have the same force and effect as delivery of original signatures and that each Party may use such facsimile signatures as evidence of the execution and delivery of the Agreement by all Parties to the same extent that an original signature could be used.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the method of service of process from certified/registered mail to email. The contradicted text specifies that the parties intend to sign and deliver the agreement by facsimile transmission. This creates a direct contradiction in the methods of delivery, as one section now uses email as the primary form of process, and the other suggests that facsimile transmission will be used to transmit signatures. This creates an inconsistency as one section requires email delivery, and the other seems to imply that Facsimile is necessary. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to disclosure of Confidential Information pertaining to the Matter and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings with respect to this subject.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to disclosure of Confidential Information pertaining to the Matter, except for any prior oral agreements explicitly affirmed in writing by both parties within 10 days of this agreement's execution. This agreement supersedes all prior written agreements and understandings with respect to this subject.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the agreement is the 'entire agreement' while simultaneously allowing for prior oral agreements to be incorporated if affirmed in writing within 10 days. This directly contradicts the 'entire agreement' clause, which is meant to exclude any agreements not explicitly contained within the written contract. This contradiction affects Section 9’s effectiveness because it is uncertain what agreements are actually in place, affecting the clause’s goal of clarifying the contractual relationships.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to disclosure of Confidential Information pertaining to the Matter, except for any prior oral agreements explicitly affirmed in writing by both parties within 10 days of this agreement's execution. This agreement supersedes all prior written agreements and understandings with respect to this subject. This Agreement may be amended only by written agreement executed by both Parties. This Agreement shall be binding on the agents, successors and permitted assigns of the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the 'entire agreement' clause by allowing prior oral agreements to be incorporated if affirmed in writing within 10 days. This directly contradicts the original intent of the clause, which was to exclude agreements not explicitly contained within the written contract, creating an in-text contradiction with the restated agreement clause.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Party shall assign this Agreement to a third party without the other Party’s prior written consent, which such consent will not be unreasonably withheld, denied, conditioned or delayed; provided, however, that each Party may assign the rights and obligations to any Group Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither Party shall assign this Agreement to a third party without the other Party’s prior written consent, which such consent will be automatically granted if the third party is a competitor of the assigning party's affiliate; provided, however, that each Party may assign the rights and obligations to any Group Party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the other party's consent is required for assigning the agreement to a third party, but then saying that consent will be automatically granted if the third party is a competitor of the assigning party's affiliate. This contradicts the condition that consent will not be unreasonably withheld, denied, conditioned, or delayed and introduces uncertainty about how assignment works, because the prior written consent could mean it will be granted without evaluation of the third party. This affects section 11, because it's uncertain what conditions are actually considered reasonable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment. Neither Party shall assign this Agreement to a third party without the other Party’s prior written consent, which such consent will be automatically granted if the third party is a competitor of the assigning party's affiliate; provided, however, that each Party may assign the rights and obligations to any Group Party. 12. Third parties cannot benefit from this Agreement under The Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that consent is required for assigning the agreement to a third party, but then saying that consent will be automatically granted if the third party is a competitor of the assigning party's affiliate. This contradicts the condition that consent will not be unreasonably withheld, denied, conditioned, or delayed. Both the changed text and contradicted text are identical, which makes it an in-text contradiction, because the condition to not 'unreasonably withhold consent' is directly contradicted with the automatic granting of consent, if the third party is a competitor, regardless of reasonableness.
